violations.text: Tortellini was observed thawing on shelving in ambient air temperature. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025
Boxes of food were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

The following was observed: 1) The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine was observed with biofilm. 2) The ceiling vents in the ware-washing room was observed with an accumulation of dust 3) The shared walk-in cooler vent was observed with an accumulation of dust New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025
The floors in the walk-in freezer has an accumulation of food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

The bakery prep room does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) was observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer broken down and moved to warewashing area to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024

The condensate from equipment on the ceiling in the bakery room is draining into the 3-bay sink. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. 3-bay currently not in use until condensate line is removed. All equipment and utensils to be washed, rinsed and sanitized in commissary warewashing area. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher in the KITCHEN DISH ROOM as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. (At the time of inspection, kitchen staff to use the BAKERY DISH ROOM for proper mechanical warewaashing sanitizing.) New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

At the time of inspection, the following equipment was observed to be in poor repair/in need of adjustment... - The blade to the kitchen dicer located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. The blade surface coating was observed to be chipping/flaking. At the time of inspection, the PIC removed the blade from the unit to be replaced. - The Sanitizing Cycle Temperature Display to the Kitchen Dish Machine was observed to not be working at the time of inspection. - The Sanitizing Cycle Pressure Measuring Device Gauge was observed to have a broken needle New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

At the time of inspection, the mechanical wash cycle to the Kitchen Dish Machine was observed to be operating below the specified 150 degrees Fahrenheit for the unit. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris at the time of inspection... - The Kitchen Large Mixer was observed to have an accumulation of food debris on the splatter shield - The Cookline Hood Vent System was observed to have an accumulation of dust New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023
